headlines.title: Oil Tankers Reverse Course on Hopes of Hormuz Reopening
headlines.summary: Two tankers that were heading to Africa have changed course and are now moving to the Middle East, Bloomberg reported today, citing ship-tracking data. headlines.title: Taiwan’s Energy Crisis Shows the Cost of Import Dependence
headlines.summary: The issue of energy dependence and its consequences has, in recent months, become a major topic of discussion. headlines.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Down Again, Losing 52 Million Barrels in 9 Weeks
headlines.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 8.33 million barrels in the week ending June 12. Analysts had expected a 4.5 million draw. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by a rather large 9.119 million bar

headlines.title: Egypt Clears $6 Billion in Energy Debt and Opens Door to a New Gas Boom

headlines.title: AI Power Hunger Sparks Push for Energy Efficiency
headlines.summary: The surge of AI and the data center boom have started to pose challenges to the global energy system amid soaring power demand, spiking energy bills, and a higher environmental footprint. headlines.title: Norway Moves to Extend the Life of Europe’s Most Important Oil Field
headlines.summary: Norway is moving ahead with the next phase of development at Johan Sverdrup, a project designed to help maintain production from the oil field that has become one of Europe's most important sources of crude supply. According to Equinor, new discoveries in the Johan Sverdrup area

headlines.title: Oil Prices Crash as Traders Bet the War Is Over
headlines.summary: The oil market just did something remarkable. After spending months pricing in tanker attacks, shut-in production, damaged LNG facilities, and the largest supply disruption in modern oil market history, traders are suddenly betting the crisis is ending. headlines.title: The Bottleneck Holding Back Britain's Energy Industry
headlines.summary: Alan Chang believes Britain’s energy crisis is largely self-inflicted. The founder of Fuse Energy, the $5bn (£3.72bn) energy supplier and infrastructure developer, argues that the UK already has the ingredients needed to drive investment in the sector. What it lacks, he says, is

headlines.title: Falling Oil Prices Could Ease Pressure on India’s Trade Deficit
headlines.summary: The tentative U.S.-Iran deal and the expected drop in oil prices amid increasing flows from the Middle East are set to help narrow India’s trade deficit, which held at relatively high levels in April and May due to the crude price shock. headlines.title: The $10 Billion Energy Corridor That Could Bypass Hormuz
headlines.summary: The extended disruption caused by the US-Iran conflict highlights a need for diversifying energy export routes to reduce dependency on shipping via the Strait of Hormuz.
